reasoning: The informal statement asserts that sin(π/12) is an algebraic number. In Mathlib, `IsAlgebraic ℚ x` is defined as `∃ p : ℚ[X], p ≠ 0 ∧ aeval x p = 0`, which is exactly the standard definition of "algebraic number" — a root of a nonzero polynomial with rational coefficients. The Lean statement `IsAlgebraic ℚ (sin (Real.pi/12))` places `sin(π/12)` in ℝ (using `Real.sin` and `Real.pi` as opened in the file) and asks whether it is algebraic over ℚ. This matches the informal claim exactly, with no added or dropped hypotheses and no change in quantifier structure.
